dalio.philosophy: All Weather Strategy — A diversified portfolio designed to survive any economic environment. The key is to cover all four scenarios: growth, recession, inflation, and deflation.
dalio.strategy: 30% Stocks + 40% Long Bonds + 15% Medium Bonds + 7.5% Gold + 7.5% Commodities. By mixing assets with low correlation, losses are minimized in any environment.
dalio.key_principle: "Pain + Reflection = Progress" — Learn from losses and establish principles. True success comes from a correct decision-making system.
dalio.track: +9.5% return during the 2008 financial crisis. $150B in assets under management, average 12% annual return over 30 years. The highest total return of any hedge fund in history.

buffett.philosophy: Value Investing — A long-term compounding strategy of buying below intrinsic value and holding for the long term. "If you aren't willing to own a stock for 10 years, don't even think about owning it for 10 minutes."
buffett.strategy: Concentrated investment in quality companies (AAPL, OXY, BRK, etc.). Always maintain 25% cash. The "Circle of Competence" principle of never investing in businesses you don't understand.
buffett.key_principle: "Be fearful when others are greedy, and greedy when others are fearful." Contrarian investing that turns market fear into opportunity.
buffett.track: +19.8% average annual return over the past 58 years. $10,000 invested in 1964 is now worth $140 million. 3x excess return vs. S&P 500.

cathie_wood.philosophy: Disruptive Innovation Investing — Focused on technologies that will change the world in 5 years: AI, blockchain, genomics, space, and more. Short-term volatility is the price of innovation.
cathie_wood.strategy: Concentrated growth stocks (TSLA, COIN, ROKU, PATH, etc.). Minimize diversification; increase position size as conviction grows. Long-term holding principle of 5+ years.
cathie_wood.key_principle: "Innovation doesn't grow linearly, it grows exponentially." Today's expensive stock can become tomorrow's bargain.
cathie_wood.track: ARK Innovation ETF +152% in 2020. Long-term AI optimism, Bitcoin $1.5M price target.

druckenmiller.philosophy: Macro Trends + Concentrated Bets — Read macroeconomic flows and bet big in one direction when conviction is high. "Betting passively on the right judgment is meaningless."
druckenmiller.strategy: Top-down macro: analyze interest rates, currencies, fiscal policy → identify high-probability asymmetric opportunities → leveraged concentrated bets. Cut losses immediately if wrong; add more if right.
druckenmiller.key_principle: "The key is not how often you are right or wrong, but how much you make when you are right." The asymmetry of returns is more important than win rate.
druckenmiller.track: 30 consecutive years of average +30% returns, never a negative year. Over $1B profit from shorting the British pound with Soros in 1992. $12B in assets when Duquesne closed.

kostolany.guide_intro: André Kostolany (1906-1999) was a legendary European investor who explained the relationship between interest rates and the stock market using an
kostolany.guide_core_text: When rates rise, stocks fall; when rates fall, stocks rise. This cycle repeats clockwise around the egg (ellipse).
kostolany.guide_bull_steps: A1 Correction (Buy) → A2 Momentum (Hold) → A3 Overheat (Sell signal)
kostolany.guide_bear_steps: B1 Correction (Take profit) → B2 Decline (Defense) → B3 Oversold (Bottom)
kostolany.guide_quote: "Buy when everyone sells, sell when everyone buys"
kostolany.evidence_empty:
Preparing data
Updated daily at 7 AM
kostolany.disclaimer: The Kostolany model is a reference indicator and not a basis for investment decisions. Please consult a professional before investing.

help.title: Help
help.faq_a1: baln is an investment education app where you build your own investment principles by reading daily 5-minute market context, participating in predictions, and reviewing outcomes. It is not a trading app.
help.faq_a2: The Context Card helps you understand why your assets moved today through 4-layer analysis (historical context, macro chain, institutional behavior, portfolio impact) in 5 minutes. It auto-updates every 3 hours.
help.faq_q3: How do I participate in the prediction game?
help.faq_a3: Vote YES/NO on 3 daily market prediction questions. Review results and explanations the next day. Earn 3 acorns for correct predictions.
help.faq_a4: 1 acorn = ₩100. Earn for free via check-in (2 acorns), correct prediction (3 acorns), sharing (5 acorns). Spend in the marketplace for extra AI analysis (1 acorn), Premium trial (5 acorns), etc.
help.faq_q5: How does the AI health checkup work?
help.faq_a5: AI analyzes 7 factors including diversification, volatility, and concentration, rating your portfolio from A+ to F. It also provides a prescription with specific improvement steps.
help.faq_a6: AI analyzes the philosophy of world-class investors like Warren Buffett, Ray Dalio, Cathie Wood, and Stanley Druckenmiller, and provides daily insights applied to today's market.
help.faq_a7: We operate on a Zero-Knowledge principle. We do not collect brokerage passwords or account numbers. All data is encrypted with TLS 1.3 + AES-256.
help.faq_q8: What are the benefits of a Premium subscription?
help.faq_a8: Full 4-layer context card analysis, prediction explanations + review, 3 AI diagnoses/day, institutional behavior analysis, full peer comparison, and 30 acorns monthly bonus. ₩4,900/month.
help.feedback_desc: You can submit feedback via Settings > Help

privacy.policy_zero_knowledge_desc: We do not collect or store your actual asset holdings, trading passwords, or brokerage login credentials. The app analyzes screenshot images only, and original images are deleted immediately after processing.
privacy.policy_encryption_desc: All data is encrypted in transit using TLS 1.3 and stored with AES-256 encryption. Encryption keys are generated and managed solely on your device.
privacy.policy_local_desc: All calculations and analysis are performed on-device wherever possible. Only anonymized data is sent to our servers when AI analysis is required.
privacy.policy_anon_desc: Data sent for AI analysis has all personally identifiable information removed. Only ticker symbols and quantities are used — no account numbers or real names are ever transmitted.
privacy.policy_delete_desc: You can delete all your data at any time. Go to Settings > Security > Delete Account to permanently erase all data. Deleted data cannot be recovered.
privacy.policy_no_share_desc: Your data is never sold or shared for advertising, marketing, or any other third-party purpose. Even in the event of a legal request, only encrypted data exists, making meaningful disclosure impossible.
privacy.purpose_body:
We process personal data for the following purposes. Data will not be used for any purpose beyond those listed below. If the purpose changes, we will obtain separate consent as required under applicable data protection law (including GDPR and CCPA).

1. Account registration & management: verifying intent to register, identifying and authenticating users, maintaining membership, preventing service misuse
2. Service delivery: portfolio management, AI-powered asset analysis, rebalancing recommendations, context card delivery, prediction game operation
3. Marketing & analytics: providing event information and promotional content (with consent), analyzing usage statistics and access frequency
4. Customer support: verifying the identity of complainants, investigating inquiries, communicating findings, and notifying outcomes
privacy.ai_decision_body:
We perform automated AI analysis (risk assessment, rebalancing suggestions, tax calculations, guru insights, etc.) based on your portfolio data.

Under applicable data protection law (including GDPR Article 22), you have the right to request an explanation of, or to object to, any fully automated decision-making.

• Opt out of AI analysis: Settings > Security > Disable AI Auto-Analysis
• Request an explanation: contact baln.logic@gmail.com

AI analysis never executes investment decisions automatically — all final decisions remain entirely yours.
privacy.cross_border_detail:
■ Data sent to Google Gemini AI:
• Portfolio ticker symbols and weights (e.g., AAPL 30%)
• Market data (prices, exchange rates, indices)
• Health score and risk factors

■ Data never sent:
• Name, email, account numbers, or other personally identifiable information
• Actual holdings amounts or transaction history

■ Safeguards:
• All data is transmitted with TLS 1.3 encryption
• Google Gemini API does not use your data to train its models (Google Cloud API Terms of Service)
• You can withdraw consent for AI data sharing in Settings at any time

All sub-processors hold GDPR compliance certifications and SOC 2 Type II attestation.
privacy.rights_body:
Under applicable data protection law (including GDPR and CCPA), you have the following rights:

1. Right to access your personal data
2. Right to correct or delete your personal data
3. Right to restrict processing
4. Right to object to automated decision-making

How to exercise your rights:
• In-app: Settings > Security > Delete Account (processed immediately)
• Email: privacy@baln.app (within 10 business days)
• Mail: Contact our Data Protection Officer at the address below
privacy.dpo_intro: In accordance with applicable data protection regulations, we have designated a Data Protection Officer (DPO) responsible for overseeing personal data processing and handling data subject complaints and remediation.
privacy.deletion_body: We delete personal data without delay once it is no longer necessary — i.e., once the retention period expires or the processing purpose is fulfilled.
privacy.deletion_procedure_body: Information entered during registration is moved to a separate database after its purpose is fulfilled and retained for the period required by internal policy or applicable law, then permanently deleted. Data in the separate database is not used for any purpose other than those prescribed by law.
privacy.deletion_method_body:
• Electronic files: deleted using technical methods that prevent restoration; database records are destroyed in a non-recoverable manner (logical deletion followed by physical overwrite).
  • Paper documents: shredded or incinerated.
  • AI analysis logs: automatically and permanently deleted 30 days after creation.
  • Backup data: purged from backup copies within 30 days of deleting the original.
privacy.deletion_legal_body:
• Records of contracts or withdrawal of offers: 5 years (applicable e-commerce law)
  • Records of payment and supply of goods/services: 5 years (applicable e-commerce law)
  • Records of consumer complaints or dispute resolution: 3 years (applicable e-commerce law)
  • Communication confirmation records: 12 months (applicable communications privacy law)
  • Login records: 3 months (applicable communications privacy law)
privacy.security_intro: We implement the following technical, administrative, and physical safeguards to protect personal data in accordance with applicable law.
privacy.security_admin_body:
• Establishment and implementation of an internal data management plan
  • Minimizing personnel with access to personal data and providing regular training
  • Periodic internal security audits
privacy.security_tech_body:
• Encryption: all data transmitted via TLS 1.3 and stored with AES-256 encryption
  • Anti-hacking measures: intrusion detection systems, security software installation and updates
  • Access control: management of access privileges to data processing systems and retention of access logs
  • Access log retention and tamper prevention: access logs kept for at least 1 year
privacy.security_physical_body: • As a cloud-based service, physical servers are managed by Supabase Inc. (AWS infrastructure) and meet SOC 2 Type II certification standards.
privacy.auto_collect_intro: We may use technologies that store and retrieve usage information to deliver personalized services.
privacy.auto_collect_items_body:
• App usage statistics (screen views, feature usage frequency)
  • Device information (OS version, app version)
  • Authentication tokens (to maintain login state)
privacy.auto_collect_purpose_body:
• Service usage analysis and improvement
  • Personalized service delivery
  • Maintaining login state
privacy.auto_collect_opt_out_body:
• You can clear app data in your device settings or reinstall the app to reset automatically collected information.
  • Note: opting out of automatic data collection may limit certain features such as staying logged in.
privacy.dispute_line1: If you believe your data protection rights have been violated, you have the right to lodge a complaint with your local data protection authority.
privacy.dispute_line2: EU/EEA users: contact your national data protection supervisory authority (e.g., the Irish DPC at dataprotection.ie).
privacy.dispute_line3: California residents (CCPA): contact the California Privacy Protection Agency at oag.ca.gov/privacy.
privacy.dispute_line4: You may also contact us directly at privacy@baln.app before filing a formal complaint.
privacy.changes_body:
1. This Privacy Policy is effective as of February 21, 2026.

2. Previous versions of this Privacy Policy:
  • This is the initial version; no prior versions exist.

3. If this Privacy Policy is updated, we will notify you via in-app notice at least 7 days before the changes take effect. For material changes affecting your rights, we will provide at least 30 days' notice.

drift.summary: Measures how far each asset's weight has drifted from your original purchase or target allocation.
drift.when_high:
Each asset is within 5% of its target weight.
Your portfolio is running as originally planned.
drift.when_low:
You haven't set target weights yet, or certain assets have moved significantly.
⚠️ Score is 0 if no target allocation is set.
drift.formula:
Sum of |actual weight − target weight| for each asset ÷ 2
→ 100 pts if drift is 0%, 0 pts if drift ≥ 25%
drift.data_source: Your manually entered asset prices + target weights (%) you've set per asset
drift.tip: Enter target weights (%) in the Analysis tab detail view to raise this score.

concentration.summary: Measures whether risk is spread across assets rather than concentrated in any single one.
concentration.when_high:
No single asset accounts for more than 40% of total risk.
Even if one asset crashes, the overall damage is limited.
concentration.when_low:
One asset is carrying more than half your total portfolio risk.
If that asset drops 30%, your entire portfolio is significantly impacted.
concentration.formula:
HHI concentration index of each asset's risk contribution (value × volatility)
→ 100 pts when evenly spread, 0 pts when fully concentrated
concentration.data_source:
Your entered asset prices + standard volatility by asset type
(e.g. Korean stocks 30%, crypto 80%, bonds 5%)
concentration.tip: Trim the largest position and add bonds, cash, or other asset classes.

correlation.summary: Measures whether your assets move in different directions. The more independently they move, the better the diversification.
correlation.when_high:
Assets move independently — like bonds rising when stocks fall.
Some assets act as a buffer when markets turn.
correlation.when_low:
All your assets move in the same direction.
Holding only stocks, crypto, and growth names means everything rises and falls together.
correlation.formula:
Weighted average of historical correlations between asset types by weight
→ 100 pts if avg correlation ≤ −0.3, 0 pts if ≥ 0.8
correlation.data_source:
Historical correlation coefficients by asset-type pair (built-in reference values)
Examples: Stocks↔Bonds −0.1, Stocks↔Crypto +0.5, Stocks↔Gold +0.1
correlation.tip: Add assets that tend to move opposite to stocks — bonds, gold, or cash.

volatility.summary: Measures how much your total portfolio value fluctuates.
volatility.when_high:
Annual price swings are under 18%.
Your portfolio holds relatively stable even during market turbulence.
volatility.when_low:
Annual price swings exceed 30%.
High crypto or growth stock exposure creates psychological pressure and panic-sell risk.
volatility.formula:
Weighted average of annual volatility per asset type × holding weight
→ 100 pts if < 18%, 0 pts if ≥ 30%
volatility.data_source:
Standard annual volatility by asset type (built-in reference values)
Examples: Korean stocks 30% / bonds 5% / crypto 80% / cash 0%
volatility.tip: Reduce crypto or individual growth stock exposure; increase bond ETFs and cash to lower swings.

downside.summary: Measures how many of your assets are currently at a loss.
downside.when_high:
Few or no assets are at a loss.
Most of your portfolio is at or above your cost basis.
downside.when_low:
A significant portion of your portfolio is sitting at a loss.
Further declines can compound losses.
downside.formula:
Weighted average of (cost basis − current price) ÷ cost basis × holding weight for losing assets
→ 100 pts if no losses, 0 pts if weighted loss ≥ 33%
downside.data_source: Your manually entered cost basis (purchase price) + current asset value
downside.tip: For a long-term strategy, consider averaging down. For shorter-term, set a clear stop-loss level.

tax.summary: Measures how well you're utilizing tax-loss harvesting opportunities.
tax.when_high:
No assets are down more than 5%, or you've already harvested losses.
Your tax efficiency is optimized.
tax.when_low:
Several assets are down more than 5%.
Selling them before year-end can offset gains elsewhere and reduce your tax bill.
tax.formula:
Number of assets down ≥ 5% ÷ total liquid assets
→ 100 pts if no harvesting opportunities, 0 pts if more than half qualify
tax.data_source: Your manually entered cost basis + current asset value
tax.tip: Sell losing assets then immediately buy similar ones — you keep your position while capturing the tax benefit.

leverage.summary: Measures whether debt or leverage is being used at a safe level.
leverage.when_high:
No debt, or LTV is below 60% on stable assets (e.g. real estate).
No forced liquidation risk even if values decline.
leverage.when_low:
You're using leverage on high-volatility assets (crypto, growth stocks).
A sharp drop could trigger forced liquidation (margin call).
leverage.formula:
LTV × asset volatility × asset value, summed across holdings ÷ total portfolio
→ 100 pts if risk metric < 10%, approaches 0 as it rises
leverage.data_source: Your entered loan-to-value ratio + asset price + standard volatility per asset type
leverage.tip: Only use leverage on stable assets like real estate. Leveraged volatile assets carry extreme risk.

large_cap.title: 📈 Stocks (Large Cap)
large_cap.role: The growth engine of your portfolio. Participates in corporate earnings — a productive asset.
large_cap.dalio: Dalio: "Strongest in growth + stable inflation environments. But can drop 50%+ in recession — diversification is essential."
large_cap.buffett: Buffett: "Stocks are the greatest asset class. Put 90% into an S&P 500 index fund. Hold 10+ years and you almost certainly win."
large_cap.when_good: During economic expansion, stable interest rates, strong corporate earnings
large_cap.when_bad: During recession, rapid rate hikes, credit crises (2008: −50%, 2022: −25%)
large_cap.tip: Both agree on holding stocks. Maintain 40% target. Buffett would go higher, but Dalio says 40% is right for diversification.

bond.title: 🏛️ Bonds
bond.role: The safety net of your portfolio. Tends to rise when stocks fall.
bond.dalio: Dalio: "The king in recession and deflation. The cornerstone of All Weather — 55% recommended."
bond.buffett: Buffett: "Bonds are a terrible investment right now. When inflation hits, bond investors face a double loss — taxes and purchasing power."
bond.when_good: During recession, deflation, falling interest rates (rates ↓ = bond prices ↑)
bond.when_bad: During inflation, rapid rate spikes (2022: bonds −17%, historically worst)
bond.tip: Dalio wants 55%, Buffett wants to minimize. Compromise: 15% — hold only as a buffer in extreme downturns.

bitcoin.title: ₿ Bitcoin
bitcoin.role: Digital gold. Capped supply (21M) makes it an inflation hedge + high-risk growth asset.
bitcoin.dalio: Dalio (2024): "It's reasonable to hold a small amount of Bitcoin. Recognizing it as a digital store of value."
bitcoin.buffett: Buffett: "Rat poison squared. It produces nothing. I wouldn't buy it for $100." — Position unchanged as of 2024.
bitcoin.when_good: Abundant liquidity, inflation fears, institutional adoption
bitcoin.when_bad: Sharp rate hikes, regulatory crackdowns, broad market panic (high beta)
bitcoin.tip: The biggest disagreement between them. Dalio: 1–3% vs Buffett: 0%. Practical compromise: 10% — decide based on your risk tolerance.

altcoin.title: 🪙 Altcoins
altcoin.role: High-risk, high-reward speculative assets. 3–5× more volatile than Bitcoin.
altcoin.dalio: Dalio: "No direct statement. But speculative assets should be strictly capped at under 5% of total."
altcoin.buffett: Buffett: "I don't invest in things I don't understand. Altcoins are no different from a casino. They produce nothing."
altcoin.when_good: Bull markets, post-Bitcoin rally altcoin seasons
altcoin.when_bad: Bear markets — drop far harder than Bitcoin (90%+ losses possible)
altcoin.tip: Both strongly oppose. Stick to a 5% ceiling — use only for tax-loss harvesting when in the red.

gold.title: 🥇 Gold / Precious Metals
gold.role: 5,000 years of value storage. Rises on dollar weakness, inflation, and geopolitical crises.
gold.dalio: Dalio: "Every portfolio should have gold. It's the only real money that isn't printed. The ultimate hedge against inflation and crisis."
gold.buffett: Buffett: "Gold produces nothing. Just sits in a vault. For the same money you could buy all US farmland or ExxonMobil — I'd never buy gold."
gold.when_good: Inflation, weak dollar, geopolitical crises, central bank distrust (strongest in stagflation)
gold.when_bad: Strong dollar, real interest rate spikes (gold pays no yield — opportunity cost rises)
gold.tip: Dalio wins this one. Buffett's 'no production' critique is valid, but diversification and crisis-hedge value is proven. Keep 12%.

commodity.title: 🛢️ Commodities
commodity.role: Assets that directly reflect inflation. Includes oil, agricultural goods, and metals.
commodity.dalio: Dalio All Weather: "Hold 7.5% in commodities. When inflation hits, commodities protect the portfolio."
commodity.buffett: Buffett: "I don't buy commodity ETFs. But I hold energy companies (Chevron, Occidental) — their earnings matter more than the commodity itself."
commodity.when_good: Inflation, supply shocks, weak dollar, geopolitical conflicts
commodity.when_bad: Recession / deflation (falling demand → commodity prices drop)
commodity.tip: Buffett prefers energy stocks over ETFs. Commodity ETFs (PDBC, DJP) are the Dalio approach — target 8%.

cash.title: 💵 Cash
cash.role: Ammunition for opportunity. Funds for buying dips. Ensures short-term liquidity.
cash.dalio: Dalio: "Cash is trash. Held long-term, inflation eats away its value — keep only the minimum."
cash.buffett: Buffett: "Berkshire always keeps at least $20B in cash. The person with cash when fear is at its peak wins."
cash.when_good: Just before a market crash, waiting for investment opportunities, ahead of planned expenses
cash.when_bad: During inflation (real purchasing power of cash erodes every year)
cash.tip: Buffett wins. Keep 10% as opportunity ammunition. Dalio wants to minimize, but 10% is needed to buy during crashes.

realestate.title: 🏠 Real Estate
realestate.role: Illiquid long-term asset. The foundation of your portfolio — not a rebalancing target.
realestate.dalio: Dalio: "Hard assets act as a buffer in financial crises. Preserves purchasing power in inflation — but LTV management is key."
realestate.buffett: Buffett: "Real estate is a fine investment but not my specialty. Great company stocks beat direct real estate. Buying a home is an excellent financial decision."
realestate.when_good: Inflation, low interest rates, population growth areas (preserves real value)
realestate.when_bad: Rapid rate hikes, population decline, credit crises (high LTV risks forced sale)
realestate.tip: Both agree on owning your primary home. Exclude from liquid asset rebalancing and manage separately. Keep LTV below 60%.

scenarios.agi.chain2: Mass automation of white-collar jobs begins

scenarios.agi.action2: Consider reducing exposure to pure labor-dependent companies
scenarios.agi.action3: Robotics, automation, power infrastructure are AGI-era essentials
scenarios.agi.action4: Distinguish companies leveraging AI vs. being replaced by AI
scenarios.agi.action5: Prepare your own career for AI-leveraged direction

Mass unemployment, consumer spending decline, recession fears. Meanwhile AI infrastructure/platform/robotics companies surge. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.

scenarios.dollarCollapse.histEvent: Nixon Shock - End of Bretton Woods (1971)
scenarios.dollarCollapse.histDrop: Dollar lost 30% vs gold in 2 years, inflation doubled

scenarios.dollarCollapse.action3: Companies with global revenue streams are natural hedges
scenarios.dollarCollapse.action4: Bitcoin's 'digital gold' narrative strengthens in this scenario
scenarios.dollarCollapse.aiPrompt: Dollar collapse scenario: BRICS gold-backed currency, end of petrodollar, DXY -30%, US inflation 12%+, gold and Bitcoin surge as alternative reserves. Central banks dump Treasuries. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.
scenarios.oilShock.title: What if oil hits $300?
scenarios.oilShock.subtitle: Middle East conflict triggers oil supply crisis
scenarios.oilShock.shareTitle: What happens to my portfolio if oil prices triple?
scenarios.oilShock.chain1: Iran-Saudi conflict escalates, Strait of Hormuz blocked
scenarios.oilShock.chain2: 20% of global oil supply disrupted overnight
scenarios.oilShock.chain3: Oil surges from $80 to $250-300 per barrel
scenarios.oilShock.chain4: Airlines, shipping, and logistics costs triple
scenarios.oilShock.chain5: Global inflation spikes to 10%+, recession fears
scenarios.oilShock.chain6: Renewable energy and nuclear investment fast-tracked
scenarios.oilShock.histDrop: S&P 500 -48% over 21 months, oil 4x in 3 months
scenarios.oilShock.histRecovery: Markets recovered in 2 years, energy independence became priority
scenarios.oilShock.histLesson: The 1973 oil shock caused the worst bear market in decades, but it also birthed the modern energy efficiency movement. Oil shocks are devastating short-term but accelerate long-term energy transitions.
scenarios.oilShock.action1: Energy stocks are the clear hedge; consider 10-15% allocation
scenarios.oilShock.action2: Airlines and travel stocks get hit hardest; reduce if overweight
scenarios.oilShock.action3: Nuclear and renewables benefit from policy acceleration
scenarios.oilShock.action4: Oil shocks are temporary; they create buying opportunities in hit sectors
scenarios.oilShock.aiPrompt: Oil price shock: Strait of Hormuz blocked, oil to $250-300, airlines devastated, global inflation 10%+, energy stocks surge, nuclear fast-tracked. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.
scenarios.supplyChain.title: What if global trade stops?
scenarios.supplyChain.subtitle: Simultaneous port strikes + cyberattack paralyzes logistics
scenarios.supplyChain.shareTitle: What if global supply chains collapse completely?
scenarios.supplyChain.chain1: Coordinated cyberattack hits major global ports (LA, Rotterdam, Shanghai)
scenarios.supplyChain.chain3: Semiconductor, auto, and electronics production halts
scenarios.supplyChain.chain4: Retail shelves empty, consumer panic buying
scenarios.supplyChain.chain5: Inflation spikes from supply shock, not demand
scenarios.supplyChain.histRecovery: Normalized over 18 months as ports cleared and demand cooled
scenarios.supplyChain.histLesson: The 2021 supply crisis taught us that just-in-time manufacturing is fragile. Companies that diversified suppliers and built inventory outperformed. The crisis accelerated reshoring trends.
scenarios.supplyChain.action1: Companies with diversified supply chains weather these storms better
scenarios.supplyChain.action2: Cybersecurity is a long-term beneficiary of infrastructure attacks
scenarios.supplyChain.action3: Avoid retailers heavily dependent on single-country imports
scenarios.supplyChain.action4: Reshoring beneficiaries (domestic manufacturing) gain from policy response
scenarios.supplyChain.aiPrompt: Global supply chain collapse: major ports cyberattacked, shipping rates 10x, auto and electronics production halted, retail shelves empty, inflation spikes. Reshoring and cybersecurity benefit. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.
scenarios.usBankCrisis.title: What if US banks fail again?
scenarios.usBankCrisis.subtitle: Commercial real estate triggers regional bank cascade
scenarios.usBankCrisis.shareTitle: What happens to my portfolio if US banks start failing?
scenarios.usBankCrisis.chain1: Commercial real estate defaults surge (office vacancy 30%+)
scenarios.usBankCrisis.chain2: 3+ regional banks fail within a week (SVB repeat)
scenarios.usBankCrisis.chain3: Deposit flight to big banks and money markets
scenarios.usBankCrisis.chain5: Credit tightens, small business lending freezes
scenarios.usBankCrisis.chain6: Fed pivots to rate cuts, markets eventually rally
scenarios.usBankCrisis.upSector1: Big Tech (flight to quality: AAPL, MSFT)
scenarios.usBankCrisis.histRecovery: S&P recovered in 2 months; regional banks took 6+ months
scenarios.usBankCrisis.histLesson: The 2023 bank run showed how fast deposits can flee in the digital age. But the Fed backstop prevented contagion. Large banks and quality tech actually benefited from the flight to safety.
scenarios.usBankCrisis.action1: Avoid concentrated positions in regional bank stocks
scenarios.usBankCrisis.action2: Quality mega-caps benefit from 'flight to safety'
scenarios.usBankCrisis.action3: Bank crises often trigger Fed pivots; be ready to buy quality on dips
scenarios.usBankCrisis.action4: Keep deposits within FDIC limits across multiple institutions
scenarios.usBankCrisis.aiPrompt: US regional bank crisis: commercial real estate defaults trigger multiple bank failures, deposit flight, credit freeze. Fed emergency response, rate cuts expected. Flight to big tech and gold. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.
scenarios.japanDebt.title: What if Japan's debt bomb explodes?
scenarios.japanDebt.subtitle: 260% debt-to-GDP finally breaks the bond market
scenarios.japanDebt.shareTitle: What happens to global markets if Japan's government bonds crash?
scenarios.japanDebt.chain1: Japanese government bond (JGB) auction fails for first time
scenarios.japanDebt.chain2: BOJ forced to choose: print more or let rates surge
scenarios.japanDebt.chain3: Yen crashes to 200+ per dollar, or rates spike to 5%+
scenarios.japanDebt.chain4: Japanese banks face massive bond losses (SVB-style)
scenarios.japanDebt.chain5: Global bond markets contagion, carry trade unwind 10x larger than Aug 2024
scenarios.japanDebt.chain6: G7 emergency coordination to stabilize markets
scenarios.japanDebt.histRecovery: ECB 'whatever it takes' in 2012, full recovery in 2-3 years
scenarios.japanDebt.histLesson: Japan's debt bomb has been predicted for 30 years and hasn't exploded because BOJ buys its own bonds. But the risk is real: if bond market confidence breaks, the contagion would be global. Central bank coordination is the key variable.
scenarios.japanDebt.action1: Gold is the ultimate hedge against sovereign debt crises
scenarios.japanDebt.action2: Reduce exposure to Japanese equities and yen-denominated assets
scenarios.japanDebt.action3: Central banks will coordinate; the panic is the buying opportunity
scenarios.japanDebt.action4: Diversify across multiple currencies and geographies
scenarios.japanDebt.aiPrompt: Japan debt crisis: JGB auction fails, BOJ credibility crisis, yen crashes or rates spike, Japanese banks face massive losses, global bond contagion, carry trade unwind. G7 emergency response. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.
scenarios.indiaPakistan.title: What if India-Pakistan escalates to nuclear?
scenarios.indiaPakistan.subtitle: Kashmir conflict spirals into nuclear standoff
scenarios.indiaPakistan.shareTitle: What happens to global markets if India-Pakistan goes nuclear?
scenarios.indiaPakistan.chain1: Major terrorist attack triggers India-Pakistan military confrontation
scenarios.indiaPakistan.chain3: Global markets crash on nuclear war fears
scenarios.indiaPakistan.chain4: India's $3.5T economy disrupted, outsourcing industry paralyzed
scenarios.indiaPakistan.chain5: Oil surges (Strait of Hormuz proximity), gold spikes
scenarios.indiaPakistan.chain6: UN/US mediation de-escalates, markets rebound sharply
scenarios.indiaPakistan.histRecovery: Full recovery in 1 week after de-escalation confirmed
scenarios.indiaPakistan.histLesson: Previous India-Pakistan confrontations have been brief because both sides understand nuclear escalation is mutual destruction. Markets crash on fear but recover fast once diplomacy prevails.
scenarios.indiaPakistan.action1: Gold is the #1 hedge for nuclear escalation scenarios
scenarios.indiaPakistan.action2: Reduce India-specific exposure if concentrated there
scenarios.indiaPakistan.action3: IT outsourcing disruption could benefit US domestic tech services
scenarios.indiaPakistan.action4: These crises historically resolve fast; panic selling is the worst response
scenarios.indiaPakistan.aiPrompt: India-Pakistan nuclear escalation: military confrontation after terrorist attack, nuclear forces mobilized, Indian economy disrupted, global risk-off. Gold and defense surge, Indian/EM equities crash. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.
scenarios.sovereignDebt.title: What if multiple countries default?
scenarios.sovereignDebt.subtitle: Global sovereign debt crisis spreads from emerging markets to G7
scenarios.sovereignDebt.shareTitle: What if the global sovereign debt house of cards collapses?
scenarios.sovereignDebt.chain1: Emerging markets (Argentina, Turkey, Egypt) default simultaneously
scenarios.sovereignDebt.chain2: Contagion spreads to Italy and UK (high debt-to-GDP)
scenarios.sovereignDebt.chain3: Global bond yields spike, debt servicing costs explode
scenarios.sovereignDebt.chain4: IMF overwhelmed, cannot bail out multiple countries
scenarios.sovereignDebt.chain5: Austerity measures trigger social unrest globally
scenarios.sovereignDebt.chain6: Central banks forced into coordinated money printing, gold surges
scenarios.sovereignDebt.histRecovery: Draghi's 'whatever it takes' (2012) turned the tide, full recovery in 3 years
scenarios.sovereignDebt.histLesson: The Eurozone crisis showed that sovereign defaults are political events. Central banks can always print money to stop the contagion — the question is political will. Those who bought during peak fear earned 100%+ returns.
scenarios.sovereignDebt.action1: Gold and hard assets are the ultimate sovereign default hedge
scenarios.sovereignDebt.action2: Avoid concentrated exposure to any single country's bonds
scenarios.sovereignDebt.action3: Central banks always intervene eventually; the panic is temporary
scenarios.sovereignDebt.action4: Quality US mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT) act as 'new safe havens'
scenarios.sovereignDebt.aiPrompt: Global sovereign debt crisis: multiple emerging markets default, contagion to Italy and UK, global bond yields spike, IMF overwhelmed, coordinated central bank money printing. Gold and Bitcoin surge. Analyze impact on each portfolio holding.
